commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram target writeback so config and cron target rewrites require explicit `operator.admin` gateway scope, with tests for missing scopes and authorized writes.

PR surface: Source +17. Total +17 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: on current main, calling `maybePersistResolvedTelegramTarget` with a legacy Telegram target and no `gatewayClientScopes` bypasses the array-only guard and can reach config/cron writeback. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the PR has a protected maintainer label, mock-only proof, and a compatibility-sensitive fail-closed behavior change; there is no narrow automated repair to request.

target.title: fix(telegram): require admin for target writeback [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Requires Telegram target writeback to have an explicit `operator.admin` gateway scope before mutating saved config or cron delivery targets.
- Keeps the numeric-target no-op path unchanged and preserves target rewrite behavior for authorized callers.
- Adds regression coverage for missing gateway scopes so absent scope metadata is denied the same way as non-admin scopes.

AI-assisted: yes.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes none.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related maintainer-requested hardening for Telegram target writeback authorization.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram target writeback no longer treats missing gateway scope metadata as permission to rewrite stored targets.
- Real environment tested: Local source checkout with focused Vitest coverage.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): 1 test file passed, 38 tests passed.
- Observed result after fix: Missing, empty, and non-admin gateway scopes skip config and cron writeback; `operator.admin` still allows the rewrite.
- What was not tested: Live Telegram API behavior was not exercised.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The change is covered at the writeback boundary with mocked config and cron stores.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Existing guard only skipped when a provided scope array lacked `operator.admin`, leaving absent scope metadata outside the denial path.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added a target-writeback test that denies missing gateway scopes and updated successful writeback cases to pass `operator.admin` explicitly.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The new missing-scope regression would have reached config/cron writeback before this patch.

If no test was added, why not?

Not applicable.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

What is the highest-risk area?

Legacy Telegram username targets will only be persisted to numeric IDs when the caller has `operator.admin`.

How is that risk mitigated?

The mutation is bounded to the existing writeback helper, numeric-target sends remain unchanged, and authorized writeback coverage still verifies config and cron rewrites.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Run the orchestrated GHSA dry-run, review-pr, autoreview, local validation, and CI gates.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Automated PR gates and GitHub Actions are pending.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet.
